perItem.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください
毎日日本語を勉強します。
その店はとても便利だ。

proposals.title: Complete godan Volitional: append う so 飲む→飲もう (not 飲も)
proposals.rationale: GodanConjugationMap maps Volitional to only the お-row mora (む→も, く→こ, …), so ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> resolves to 飲も, not the full 飲もう. Ichidan/irregular by contrast yield the complete よう/しよう. This forces authors to append a raw literal う to finish the inflection, so conjugation correctness/idiomaticity depends on hand-spelling (r1-c1).
proposals.change: In src/verb-types.d.ts make godan Volitional emit the full ending. Either change each GodanConjugationMap entry Volitional value to the お-row mora + う (む→'もう', く→'こう', etc.), or special-case Volitional in ConjugateVerb's godan branch to append 'う' after GetGodanConjugation. Add type test ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> === '飲もう'.

proposals.title: Complete godan Passive/Causative auxiliary: 降る→降られ (not 降ら)
proposals.rationale: GodanConjugationMap returns only the あ-row stem for Passive (る→'ら') and Causative, omitting the auxiliary れ/せ, while the ichidan branch returns the full ${stem}られ. So ConjugateVerb<降る,'Passive'> is 降ら, and authors append raw 'れた' to reach 降られた, hiding the passive auxiliary and past morphemes in a literal. Affects godan passive/causative items (r1-c3 plus sibling i03/i04 cited in issues).
proposals.change: In src/verb-types.d.ts complete the godan Passive/Causative surfaces to include the auxiliary: Passive → あ-row + 'れ' (る→'られ'), Causative → あ-row + 'せ'. Do this in the map values or by special-casing those forms in ConjugateVerb's godan branch (mirroring ichidan `${stem}られ`). Add test ConjugateVerb<降る,'Passive'> === '降られ'. WARNING: this changes the contract — audit chapters that currently append れ themselves (i03/i04/r1-c3) so it is not doubled.

proposals.title: Add POS-preserving parts to the Sentence vocabulary (NaAdjective stem, Adnominal, noun subclass)
proposals.rationale: In the Sentence<[...]> path NounPart is POS-agnostic (type:'noun', value:string), so authors must demote な-adjectives (便利), 連体詞 (その), and lose common/proper/pronoun subclass into a bare noun leaf. r1-a2 (便利だ, その店) is clearest: declared aliases Adnominal<その>/CommonNoun<店>/NaAdjective 便利 are discarded because the only landing slot is NounPart. Highest-frequency D1 cluster.
proposals.change: In src/phrase-types.d.ts add: (1) AdnominalPart<A extends string> {type:'adnominal'; value:A}; (2) make NounPart carry its subclass (NounPart<N extends Noun> with value = N['word'], or add CommonNounPart/ProperNounPart/PronounPart); (3) route な-adjective predicates through AdjectivePart over a na-adjective stem + CopulaPart rather than NounPart. Add each to the PhrasePart union, then update the annotate prompt part list.

proposals.title: Forbid wrapping な-adjectives / 連体詞 in NounPart; route 便利だ through adjective+copula
proposals.rationale: The prompt already says 'never wrap a non-noun in a noun type' yet r1-a2 wrapped 便利 (な-adjective) and その (連体詞) in NounPart with the correct aliases declared-but-unused. A sharper hard rule plus a worked example prevents this high-severity D1 misclassification systemically, independent of the ts-def part work.
proposals.change: In playground/scripts/annotate.ts hard rules add: な-adjective predicates (便利だ, 静かだ) MUST be AdjectivePart over a NaAdjective stem + CopulaPart<'Plain'>, NEVER NounPart; 連体詞 (この/その/あの/どの) MUST use Adnominal/AdnominalPart, never NounPart. Add a one-line example (便利だ → AdjectivePart + CopulaPart<'Plain'>). Wire any new part names into the allowed-imports lists.

proposals.title: Add humble-polite copula form でございます to CopulaForm
proposals.rationale: CopulaForm lacks a 丁重語 member, so 営業中でございます (r1-c4) can only be a raw literal suffix instead of ConjugateCopula<'営業中','...'>. Isolated, but a small additive change that makes a real inflection expressible.
proposals.change: In src/copula-types.d.ts add a CopulaForm member (e.g. 'PoliteHumble') and matching Copula<F> branch → 'でございます'. Add test ConjugateCopula<'営業中','PoliteHumble'> === '営業中でございます'.

proposals.title: ここでお待ちします: conjugate する via ConjugateVerb<する,'Masu'> instead of literal します
proposals.rationale: します is the Masu form of irregular する, fully supported by the library, but r1-c2 froze it as a raw literal, hiding a modellable conjugation (D4 high). Pure authoring slip — fix the one snippet.
proposals.change: In the r1-c2 chapter snippet replace the trailing literal します with VerbPart<する,'Masu'> (or ${ConjugateVerb<する,'Masu'>}), keeping お as the honorific prefix literal and 待ち via the existing constructor. Resolution must stay ここでお待ちします.

proposals.title: Optional: model よう formal-noun + に to expose the buried particle in ようにする
proposals.rationale: In 早く寝るようにしてください (r1-c0) the particle に is buried inside the literal ように (D3, flagged high in one pass), and よう has no FormalNoun constructor so the formal-noun + particle decomposition is lost. Only one corpus item, but ようにする is a recurring fixed-expression class.
proposals.change: Prefer a prompt rule first: in annotate.ts direct that the に in ように be surfaced as its own ParticlePart (よう + ParticlePart<'に'> + VerbPart<する,...>) while keeping ください whole as an auxiliary. Only add a FormalNoun/形式名詞 constructor for よう in src if frequency rises.

issues.note: 早く is the adverbial (連用形) form of the i-adjective 早い, but is tagged Adverb<"早く">, freezing an inflected adjective as a lexical adverb. If the library cannot derive adjective adverbial forms, this is a ts-def limitation rather than a true lexical adverb.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ください (imperative/honorific of くださる) and the ～ように pattern are kept as raw literals. Defensible as fixed expressions, but ください in particular is a modellable auxiliary that the library does not provide a constructor for.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: The particle に in ように is hidden inside a raw string literal; the よう+に could in principle be a FormalNoun + Particle but the library likely lacks a clean よう formal-noun constructor, so に is not a visible unit.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ように collapses the formal noun よう and particle に into one leaf; no decomposition into morphemes.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ようにする pattern: prompt could guide whether ように should be split (よう + に) or kept whole; current author kept it whole as a fixed expression which is defensible but borderline.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: Particle に in ように is hidden inside a raw string literal; library has Particle/Noun constructors so よう+に could be split, but the grammar pattern よう(様)+に+する is awkward to express through the typed library, leaving the particle invisible.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: ように collapsed into one leaf; the nominal よう (様) + に decomposition is lost.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: Annotate guidance could direct splitting the よう/に of the ようにする pattern rather than freezing it as a literal, while still keeping ください whole as an auxiliary.
issues.jp: 早く寝るようにしてください

issues.note: The godan Volitational table maps ending む to only the お-row mora も (GetGodanConjugation gives 飲も, confirmed by example-verb.ts comment // 買お). The library cannot produce the complete godan volitional 飲もう, so the author must append a literal う to ConjugateVerb<飲む,"Volitional">. The surface is correct Japanese but the conjugator is incomplete for godan volitional.
Because of the same ts-def gap, the volitional ending う is a raw literal in the template instead of being modelled by the conjugator; defensible workaround but not a fully honest inflection encoding.

GetGodanConjugation maps Volitional to only the o-row mora (む→も, く→こ, etc.), unlike ichidan/irregular which return the full よう/しよう. This forces the author to append a raw う literal to complete 飲もう. The completion is correct Japanese, but the library's godan Volitional is incomplete; ideally ConjugateVerb<飲む,"Volitional"> would itself yield 飲もう.

Godan Volitional in GodanConjugationMap (verb-types.d.ts) emits only the お-row mora (む→も), not the full ending よう/もう, so ConjugateVerb<飲む,'Volitional'> resolves to 飲も. The author must append a raw literal う in the template to reach 飲もう. A complete library would have the Volitional map produce もう and the conjugator alone would render the whole inflection; the externalized う is forced by this gap, not an authoring choice.

Because the volitional ending is partly spelled as a raw literal, conjugation correctness depends on the author manually supplying う; the conjugator does not fully own the inflected surface for godan volitional.

します is the polite (Masu) form of irregular する, which the library CAN express as ConjugateVerb<する, "Masu"> (resolves to します). Leaving it as a raw literal hides a modellable inflection in this お…します humble construction.
します as an opaque literal collapses a conjugated verb into a chunk; only で is honestly decomposed while the verbal ending hides structure.

します is the Masu form of する but is written as a raw literal in the template instead of ConjugateVerb<する, 'Masu'>; the library supports irregular する conjugation, so this should go through the conjugator for fidelity.
The trailing します hides the する verb's structure as part of the literal template string rather than appearing as its own conjugated unit.

Honorific お prefix as a literal is defensible, but pairing it with a hand-spelled します undercuts the otherwise correct humble-frame analysis; ideally お${ConjugateVerb<待つ,'Masu'>}${ConjugateVerb<する,'Masu'>}.
"します" is a raw literal; the する Masu stem し is expressible via ConjugateVerb<する,"Masu">, so the conjugatable verb is frozen into a string instead of being conjugated.
The humble complex お待ちします bundles honorific prefix お + 連用形 待ち + する; only 待ち is built via a constructor, leaving お and する hidden in literals.

The honorific prefix お and the ます polite auxiliary have no dedicated constructor in the library, so the お...します humble frame can only be partly expressed; spelling them as literals is somewhat forced.

ConjugateVerb<降る, "Passive"> yields only the passive stem 降ら; the passive auxiliary れる cannot be further conjugated to its Ta form, forcing the れた tail to be hard-coded as a raw literal. The library has no way to chain Passive -> Ta, so the past-tense inflection is not expressed through the conjugator.

The れた suffix bundles the rest of the passive auxiliary (れ) plus the past marker (た) as a single opaque literal. Honest analysis would model 降る[Passive] then [Ta], but the library cannot, consistent with sibling chapters (i03/i04) that also append れた/れます/せられた as literals.

GodanVerb Passive only yields the あ-row stem (降ら → 'ら'), not the full passive auxiliary れる. The author had to append 'れた' as a raw literal to reach 降られた; the conjugator cannot compose passive れる + past た, so the auxiliary inflection is not type-driven.

Because the library lacks a stacked-auxiliary/passive-past form, れた appears as a raw string literal. A Japanese teacher would analyze 降られた as 降ら+れ+た (passive auxiliary れる + past), which the library cannot express compositionally.

ConjugateVerb<降る,'Passive'> for a godan verb returns only the a-stem 降ら (from GodanConjugationMap る.Passive='ら'), not the full passive 降られ. The auxiliary れる and the past た must be appended as the raw literal 'れた', so the passive+past inflection is not actually expressed by the conjugator. Library cannot chain the passive auxiliary れる into further conjugation.

Because of the above, the honest analysis 降る -> passive 降られる -> past 降られた is flattened: the れ (passive) and た (past) morphemes are hidden inside a string literal rather than each being a modeled conjugation step.

でございます (very-polite/humble copula = ござる+ます) is not among CopulaForm's members (Plain/Polite/Past/Written/Te/etc.), so it cannot be produced by ConjugateCopula and must be a raw literal suffix on the noun.

Ideal analysis would be ConjugateCopula<"営業中", "VeryPolite"> rather than `${営業中}でございます`; the inflection is modellable in principle but the library exposes no such form, so the raw-literal copula is forced rather than an authoring choice.

でございます is appended as an opaque literal. CopulaForm in src/copula-types.d.ts lacks a super-polite/courteous form (only Plain/Polite/...), and verb-types.d.ts has no ござる irregular (its 連用形 is ございnot ござり), so the copula+verb+ます span cannot be expressed via ConjugateCopula/ConjugateVerb. Decomposing it would require a new copula form or special verb; current frozen literal is the only honest option.

でございます is the 丁重語 (humble-polite) copula but CopulaForm has no such member, so it is appended as a raw literal rather than via ConjugateCopula; an added form (e.g. 'PoliteHumble' -> でございます) would let it be expressed as a true copula inflection.
Because the copula table omits でございます (and ござる/ございます generally), the inflection is hard-coded; with library support the honest analysis would be 営業中 + ConjugateCopula(humble-polite).
In the Sentence<[...]> path, NounPart is POS-agnostic, so the common-noun subclass of 机/上/猫 is not encoded. Inherent library limitation, used appropriately here; no authoring error.

ます is emitted as a raw SuffixPart literal rather than modelled as the polite auxiliary, because the Masu conjugation returns only the renyokei stem (勉強し) and the library offers no auxiliary-verb constructor. Minor and unavoidable; stem+ます remains linguistically honest.
issues.jp: 毎日日本語を勉強します。

issues.note: NounPart is POS-agnostic so 日本語's common-noun subclass is not encoded, but no non-noun is hidden and the analysis is correct.
issues.jp: 毎日日本語を勉強します。

issues.note: 便利 is a な-adjective (形容動詞) but is wrapped in NounPart<"便利">, tagging it as a noun. The library provides NaAdjective + AdjectivePart (e.g. AdjectivePart<{type:"na-adjective";stem:"便利"}, ...>), so this is an avoidable authoring slip, not a type-system gap. The honest analysis is na-adjective stem 便利 + plain copula だ.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: その is correctly given as Adnominal<"その"> but must be passed through NounPart, which is POS-agnostic and re-tags it as type:"noun", erasing the 連体詞 classification. No AdnominalPart exists in the phrase-part union.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: Treating the predicate adjective 便利 as a plain noun gives a learner the wrong POS picture; a teacher would mark it 形容動詞. Segmentation is otherwise idiomatic and だ is correctly split out.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: 便利 is a な-adjective but is stored as NounPart<"便利">. NounPart is POS-agnostic so it hides the NaAdjective; the library DOES model 便利 as NaAdjective (see a09/a10 chapters), so an adjective-aware part should carry it. NounPart silently demotes a 形容動詞 to a noun.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: その is an Adnominal (連体詞) and 店 a CommonNoun, but both are wrapped in POS-agnostic NounPart, losing the adnominal vs common-noun distinction even though the snippet bothers to declare type その = Adnominal and type 店 = CommonNoun (those aliases are then unused).
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: Honest analysis of 便利だ is na-adjective stem 便利 + copula だ (Plain). Treating 便利 as a noun leaf is misleading; the Sentence parts vocabulary has no NaAdjective-stem part, forcing the author into NounPart.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: だ is correctly CopulaPart<"Plain">, but because the stem is a NounPart literal rather than an adjective part, the 便利+だ predicate is not expressed through the adjective/copula conjugation machinery the library provides.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: Declared aliases type その = Adnominal<"その"> and type 店 = CommonNoun<"店"> are passed into NounPart, which only accepts a string; this both discards the POS type and is a sloppy/likely-non-typechecking use of the declared aliases as type args to a string-parameter.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: 便利 is a な-adjective (NaAdjective, べんり, na-adjective in vocab and used as NaAdjective in a09/i14) but is wrapped as NounPart<"便利"> tagged type:noun. The library exposes NaAdjective and AdjectivePart, so this is a wrong-POS authoring choice, not a missing type.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: その is correctly aliased Adnominal<"その"> but then placed in NounPart<その>, which is POS-agnostic (type:"noun") and erases the 連体詞 classification. NounPart hides a non-noun, exactly the ts-def case the rubric calls out; there is no AdnominalPart to carry it.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: 店 is built as CommonNoun<"店"> but NounPart only stores a bare string, so the common/proper/pronoun subclass is lost in the Sentence path.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。

issues.note: 便利だ is modeled as NounPart + CopulaPart instead of treating 便利 as a な-adjective stem; the copula surface is correct but the adjectival predicate analysis is not expressed.
issues.jp: その店はとても便利だ。
